ogTitle: Jinghi vs Johnsons Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jinghi and Johnsons Hill.
heading: Jinghi vs Johnsons Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jinghi, Queensland and Johnsons Hill, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Johnsons Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Jinghi (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jinghi or Johnsons Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jinghi has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Johnsons Hill scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jinghi scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Johnsons Hill scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jinghi or Johnsons Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Johnsons Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
